macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Chip Stocks Slide as AI Trade Wobbles, Dragging Global Markets
items.summary: A rotation out of recent rally winners sent chip stocks toward their steepest weekly decline in over a year, causing U.S. stock index futures to slide on Friday and Asian shares to fall as tech weakness offset energy gains.

items.title: Fed Policymakers Swell with Rate-Hike Voices Ahead of July Decision
items.summary: Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack joined a growing chorus arguing interest rates may need to rise to combat persistent inflation, setting up a charged debate at the Fed's next meeting under Chairman Kevin Warsh, though rates are still widely seen on hold.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge to One-Month High Amid US-Iran Conflict and Hormuz Threats
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel as the US completed its latest strikes on Iran and Trump announced cargo fees for ships passing the Strait of Hormuz, with the IEA chief warning the crisis threatens global energy security.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Regain Top Spot as World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple regained the world's top spot with a $4.88 trillion valuation after Nvidia saw a 3.5% market value drop, marking a significant shift in the AI-driven market leadership.

items.title: Netflix Tumbles on Weak Forecast, Adding Pressure to Tech Selloff
items.summary: Netflix shares fell sharply after issuing a weak forecast, compounding pressure on U.S. stock futures as investors reassess the sustainability of the year's AI-fueled rally.

items.title: China Holds Benchmark Lending Rates Unchanged for 14th Month Despite Soft Data
items.summary: China is set to leave benchmark lending rates unchanged in July despite softer-than-expected Q2 economic data highlighting uneven growth, while its largest private lender HDFC Bank in India reported a 5% profit rise.

items.title: US Imposes New 25% Tariffs on Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The US will charge new 25% tariffs on some Brazilian imports including beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials, though Brazil's Finance Minister stated there are no grounds for immediate retaliation.

items.title: Alphabet and Intel Earnings Set to Sway AI Trade in Coming Week
items.summary: U.S. corporate earnings season heats up with Alphabet and Intel set to offer updates that could significantly influence the market-leading AI trade as investors check if companies can meet high profit expectations.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slips Below $62K Amid US–Iran Conflict, $350M Liquidated
items.summary: Bitcoin fell over 2% to around $62,000 as escalating US–Iran military strikes triggered a sharp crypto selloff, liquidating roughly $350 million in leveraged positions across markets[8].

items.title: US Regulators Miss GENIUS Act’s One-Year Deadline for Final Stablecoin Rules
items.summary: US regulators failed to issue final stablecoin regulations by the GENIUS Act’s one-year deadline, despite the Act having legitimized stablecoins and shortened enterprise sales cycles[1][3].

items.title: Uniswap Governance Votes on v4 Fees and Robinhood Chain Expansion
items.summary: On July 19, Uniswap governance is voting to enable protocol fees for some v4 pools for the first time, with proposals covering v4 fees on seven chains and v2/v3 fees on Robinhood Chain[7].

items.title: TOSHI Token Surges 17% to $0.00012861, Market Cap Hits $54M
items.summary: TOSHI rallied sharply by 17.07% on July 18, reaching a spot price of $0.00012861 and a market cap exceeding $54 million amid broader crypto volatility[6].

items.title: FTX to Distribute $900M to Creditors in Fifth Payout Wave
items.summary: FTX announced it will distribute roughly $900 million to creditors in its fifth wave of payouts, marking a major step in the collapsed exchange’s long-term restitution process[1].

items.title: France Blocks Polymarket Over Addictive Mechanics and User Restrictions
items.summary: France ordered internet service providers to block Polymarket, citing concerns over addictive mechanics, lack of self-exclusion tools, and widespread user bypassing of prior financial restrictions[7].

items.title: Kaspersky Identifies OkoBot Malware Targeting Crypto Investors
items.summary: Kaspersky discovered OkoBot, a sophisticated new malware framework using advanced social engineering to steal cryptocurrency assets from investors[3].

items.title: Coinbase CEO Armstrong: Bitcoin Bottom Near $60K, Watch $53.6K Realized Price
items.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated Bitcoin likely bottomed around $60,000 based on the four-year halving cycle, with the key support level at the realized price of $53,600[8].

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Chinese Startup Moonshot Unveils Kimi K3, World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model
items.summary: Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8-trillion-parameter model with a 1-million-token context window, claiming performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and delivering frontier-adjacent benchmark scores.

items.title: Anthropic Restores Fable and Mythos Models, Launches Claude Sonnet 5
items.summary: Anthropic deployed Claude Sonnet 5 and restored access to its advanced Fable and Mythos frontier models after the US lifted curbs following a federal export control review.

items.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines Lab Releases First Open Model 'Inkling'
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, led by former OpenAI executive Mira Murati, released its first open model 'Inkling' to challenge one-size-fits-all AI and loosen the grip of frontier labs like OpenAI and Anthropic.

items.title: OpenAI Unveils GPT-Red, Internal Red-Teaming LLM for GPT-5.6
items.summary: OpenAI introduced GPT-Red, an automated red-teaming model that reduced prompt injection success rates on GPT-5.6 from over 90% to under 23%, significantly outperforming human red-teamers.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron 3 Embed, Top-Ranked Open Retrieval Model
items.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Embed as three open checkpoints, with its flagship 8B model scoring #1 overall on retrieval benchmarks (78.5% RTEB, 75.5% MMTEB).

items.title: Databricks Valued at $188 Billion Following $3B Coatue Investment
items.summary: Investment firm Coatue Management led a $3 billion investment in data analytics provider Databricks, valuing the company at $188 billion according to the Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia as World's Most Valuable Company Amid AI Reassessment
items.summary: Apple surpassed Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company as investors reassess the artificial intelligence outlook, reshuffling tech heavyweight rankings.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Data Center Moratorium for Up to One Year
items.summary: New York will block new large AI data centers for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid, marking the first statewide moratorium and potentially setting a blueprint for anti-AI infrastructure movements.

items.title: Apple Accuses OpenAI of Stealing Trade Secrets in Hardware Development
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of conspiring with former Apple employees to steal trade secrets as Apple seeks to build its own hardware for ChatGPT.

items.title: Japan Plans to Buy Thousands of Next-Gen Nvidia Chips for AI Ecosystem
items.summary: The Japanese government plans to purchase thousands of Nvidia's next-generation semiconductors to power its artificial intelligence push and build an AI ecosystem on its own soil.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Mistfall Hunter Gets Official July 29, 2026 Release Date
items.summary: Fantasy PvPvE action game Mistfall Hunter will launch on PC, PS5, and Xbox Series X/S on July 29, 2026, with its Steam page now live.

items.title: Bethesda Teases Fallout 5 Following Xbox Mass Layoffs
items.summary: Bethesda has teased Fallout 5 shortly after Microsoft announced mass layoffs affecting up to 50% of some teams at Bethesda and id Software.

items.title: Hundreds Protest at Bethesda HQ Over Xbox Layoffs
items.summary: Hundreds rallied at Bethesda headquarters to protest Microsoft's layoffs, with union representatives demanding a halt to the "perpetual cycle" of job cuts.

items.title: Kuro Games Debuts Wuthering Waves: Elysium Teaser
items.summary: Kuro Games used a July 18 livestream to reveal the first teaser for Wuthering Waves: Elysium and launch new animation brand Kuro Onroad.

items.title: Take-Two Signals New Major Acquisitions After Strong Fiscal Year
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive exceeded fiscal 2026 expectations and announced plans for new accretive mergers and acquisitions while investing in technology.

items.title: Nintendo Switch 2 Price Increases $50 Starting September 1
items.summary: Nintendo will raise the price of the Switch 2 by $50 later this year due to changing market conditions, effective September 1.

items.title: Fortnite Introduces AI-Powered "Personas"
items.summary: Fortnite is rolling out a new feature adding AI-powered "personas" to enhance player interactions and gameplay experiences.

items.title: Castlevania: Belmont's Curse Confirmed for Nintendo Switch in October
items.summary: New Castlevania game Castlevania: Belmont's Curse will be released on Nintendo Switch in October 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Become World's Most Valuable Company
items.summary: Apple reclaimed the top spot as the world's most valuable company on Friday, surpassing Nvidia as investors reassess the AI market outlook.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit against OpenAI and two former employees, alleging they misappropriated trade secrets to develop unrevealed AI gadgets.

items.title: Meta's Instagram and Facebook Suffer Sunday Outages
items.summary: Users reported widespread issues with Meta's Instagram and Facebook apps and websites on Sunday, indicating service disruptions across both social networks.

items.title: First National Protests Against US Data Center Buildout Held
items.summary: Opponents of rapid data center expansion held coordinated protests at over 125 locations across the US on Saturday, marking the first national effort against AI infrastructure growth.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Data Center Moratorium
items.summary: New York will block new large data centers for up to one year to protect the environment and energy grid, becoming the first state with such a moratorium.

items.title: Shanghai Company Launches First Space-Computing Satellite Constellation
items.summary: Shanghai Xingshu Tiansuan Space Technology Co. launched the first constellation of a space-computing project aiming to deploy 1,000 satellites total.

items.title: T-Mobile and Verizon Now Lock Financed iPhones to Carriers
items.summary: iPhones financed through T-Mobile and Verizon will now be permanently locked to their respective carriers, ending previous unlock flexibility.

items.title: Major League Baseball Restricts Dugout iPad Use to Prevent AI Influence
items.summary: MLB is restricting iPad usage in dugouts to prevent artificial intelligence from influencing game decisions, a move to maintain human judgment in play.

items.title: TSMC Plans $100 Billion Expansion of US Manufacturing Capacity
items.summary: Taiwan's TSMC announced plans to invest another $100 billion to expand its chip manufacturing capacity in the United States.

items.title: US Justice Department Allows TikTok on Government Devices
items.summary: The US Justice Department announced Friday that federal employees can now download and use the short-video app TikTok on government devices.
